summary: An almost flawless sound‑board capture of a spring‑1970 show highlighted by an incendiary Other One jam and crystal‑clear playing from the whole band.
review: The recording is crisp, balanced and feels like you’re sitting in the first row – most reviewers call it “soundboard quality” with no audible hiss or muddiness. Musically the night is a showcase of peak 1970 Dead: a scorching Cryptic‑Drums‑Jam‑Other One sequence, blistering Dire Wolf, soulful Lovelight and powerhouse performances of Man’s World, Candyman and Dancin’. The only blemishes are a few missing tracks (China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider) and the early fade‑out on Hard to Handle.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70 reviews overwhelmingly praise both performance and audio quality; show quality (70 %) is clearly outstanding and recording quality (30 %) is described as excellent, yielding a near‑perfect combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with balanced vocals and instruments; no noticeable tape hiss or distortion. A few reviewers note occasional cuts/fade‑outs (Hard to Handle) but overall the sound is comparable to top‑tier Dead soundboards.

standout_songs: Cryptical Envelopment > Drums > Jam > The Other One
poor_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider (missing)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert builds from a tight first set into the legendary mid‑show Cryptical/Drums jam, then erupts with an Other One suite that drives the second half. Even with missing tunes the pacing stays relentless and energetic, delivering classic 1970 spring momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for “smoking” guitar tone, deliberate creativity, and wailing leads throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for a grooving, thundering bass that anchors the Other One suite and keeps the band locked in.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned mainly for his vocal exchanges on Lovelight/NFA sandwich; some reviewers note “Bob’s histrionics” adding excitement.
band_member_comments.Keys: Few direct comments – one reviewer calls the keyboards “perfectly balanced”; another mentions extra keyboardist (third guitarist/keyboard) during the jam, but overall no complaints about the keys mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Cryptical‑Envelopment > Drum jam is repeatedly called “scorching”, “intense” and a highlight of the show.
